dialogos.texto_en: (We arrived at the Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium\n at Ocean Expo Park,\n and decided to start by looking around the area first.)
(So far, on the way here, I haven't been able to gain \n any more information that would help clue me in...)
(They can get overly excited while sightseeing \n and could potentially reveal their true selves.\n Alright, this is where it gets interesting!)
Ohhh! I knew it was big,\nbut isn't it amazing to see it in person?!
Really, it's larger than I thought!
This is beyond what I imagined.\nIt feels like we're really underwater.
I-it's almost a little scary, isn't it...
That big shadow!\nCould that be a whale shark?!?!
It's not just a whale shark.\nLook, there's also a giant manta ray\nswimming over there.
To be able to see such huge living creatures\nup close like this...!
(As expected, their excitement is starting to rise.\n There might be a clue to be found\n if I keep watching them.)
(But actually, this aquarium is really interesting.\n I'd like to take my time and look around and yet...)
Uesugi-kun, are you enjoying the sightseeing?
Eh?\nWhy are you suddenly asking me that?
You look so tense.\nMaybe try to relax a little more?
We're on an Okinawa trip after all,\nso let's not forget to have fun and make some memories.
I just can't get into that kind of mood.\nI have a deadline to meet.
Well, that may be so...
But, whose fault do you think it is?
Ggh...\nI can't come up with a retort to that.
Don't forget the original purpose\nof our graduation trip. Let's make some good memories.
Well, what you guys are saying makes sense.\nWe're on a graduation trip,\nso we might as well enjoy ourselves.
Yes! Now, let's get back to our sightseeing.
That so?\nWell, we're making memories in our own way, huh.
Are we really?
It's a little different from what I was expecting.\nShall we try to enjoy ourselves a bit more?
Come to think of it,\nit does feel a bit lacking as a graduation trip...
If it's lacking,\nthen we can make more memories from now on.
Yeah, that's right.
Well, what you guys are saying makes sense.\nWe're on a graduation trip,\nso we might as well enjoy ourselves.
Yes! Now, let's get back to our sightseeing.
That's true.\nWhen you think about it,\nthat's why we came all the way to Okinawa.
Yes, let's not forget\nthe original purpose of coming here.
Things have ended up like this, but...\nI still want to enjoy this trip as a trip.
Yeah, I feel bad for you guys,\nhaving planned it for us.
That's right, you know?\nWe put a lot of effort into planning this trip.\nI won't stand for it to go to waste.
It's not too late to make the most of it from now on.
There's still time, so let's enjoy ourselves\nand make some unforgettable memories.
Well, what you guys are saying makes sense.\nWe're on a graduation trip,\nso we might as well enjoy ourselves.
Yes! Now, let's get back to our sightseeing.
(...However, the way she's supporting me now\n feels a bit like Ichika.)
My my...!\nSo many tropical fish...!
Oh? Looks like the curator's explanation is starting.
That sounds interesting.\nShall we listen?
Haha...\nThat was kind of funny.
What was?
Well, I never expected to hear about the taste\nand traditional dishes of fish\nduring a tour of an aquarium.
Indeed, it was a bit unusual\nas a description of the fish we were watching...
Here in Okinawa, life and the sea\nare so closely intertwined. Maybe that's why?
...I see, that makes sense.\nYou really noticed that well.
Hm?\nYeah...
Teehee...
(The way this Itsuki reacted just now\n seemed like Miku...)
Come to think of it...\nAre those ingredients recommended by the locals?\nIf so, it makes me even more curious about the taste.
(This feels so much like Itsuki, though.)
Ah!\nThere's a beam of light in the water tank!
Oh wow, it's beautiful.
Is this a lighting effect?
No, there's no roof on this tank,\nso it's probably the effect of sunlight\nshining down from above.
It's shimmering and shining...\nNature is creating this kind of performance.
(This romantic atmosphere feels like Nino.)
They also fed the fish from above the tank.\nThe fish are starting to gather.
Yeah, they skillfully manipulated the baited basket\nto lure the fish into a more visible area.
It looked pretty interesting, and if we have a chance\nto experience feeding the fish ourselves,\nI'd like to give it a try.
(This active remark...might come from Yotsuba.)
(Did the advice to relax work?\n I kind of feel like\n I'm starting to understand a bit, somehow.)
(Alright...\n If I keep this up,\n I might be able to see through all of them today!)
